statement: The new persisted aggregate counters are currently correct and do not show drift on the audited dev dataset.
rationale: Exact recomputation using the same runtime completion semantics found zero mismatches across samples, runs, and experiments.
keyAssumptions: The one-off query matched the runtime completion logic closely enough to serve as a trustworthy drift check.
validationCriteria: Future spot checks continue to show zero drift under new workloads.
critiquePoints: This is a point-in-time result and does not eliminate future concurrency risk.
questions: Should a lightweight integrity audit be kept as a maintenance query after high-load reruns?
